title: Apple is escalating its lawsuit against OpenAI over AI devices, open models increasingly preferred
description:
Apple hardened its trade-secret suit against OpenAI as OpenAI called it meritless and reports described plans for a screenless voice-first speaker with a launch targeted for 2027, book publishers including Elsevier and Scott Turow sued Google over Gemini training on copyrighted works, and IBM shed more than a quarter of its value as corporate spend rotated from software to AI infrastructure.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-july-15-2026/

0:00 Apple and OpenAI escalate the trade-secret fight
0:30 Knowledge That Walks Across the Street
2:38 IBM sheds a quarter of its value
6:04 Publishers sue Google over Gemini training
8:13 The Race Moves to Open Weights
12:00 Three Scales, One Fight Over the Rules
14:23 Closing & Synthesis

This episode covers the Apple-OpenAI trade-secret escalation and OpenAI's speaker ambitions, the publishers' copyright suit against Google Gemini and Microsoft's attribution concept, IBM's 25% single-session drop with Thomson Reuters cutting 500 engineers and 26 Meta workers suing over AI-run layoffs, Chinese open-weight models accounting for 41% of measured downloads in Hugging Face's Spring 2026 report while the Gulf still can't escape Nvidia, and xAI buying a $1B gas-turbine fleet as Anthropic and OpenAI split on AI governance.

The day's signal is value coming loose at every layer. Apple is trying to fence device expertise that lives in roughly 400 engineers who can walk across the street to OpenAI, even as OpenAI is reportedly building toward a lineup of around five devices led by a screenless conversational speaker. Publishers took Google to court over books ingested into Gemini without payment, and one of the largest model-builders started publicly sketching attribution plumbing to route value back to sources. IBM's collapse, Thomson Reuters' cuts, and the Meta layoff suit describe one rotation as the software seat stops being the moat. In Hugging Face's Spring 2026 report, Chinese open weights accounted for 41% of measured downloads and held the top six spots in a recent OpenRouter token ranking, while Reflection signed a $1B Nebius deal and the chokepoint slid down to Nvidia silicon. xAI bought a gas-turbine fleet to feed Grok as utilities sought $9.2 billion in rate hikes and Pennsylvania began metering data-center water and power.
